metadata.name: lhs_2016_full_parameter
metadata.description:
Latin Hypercube Sampling sweep at 2016-block retarget for unbiased feature importance.

PURPOSE: The targeted sweeps at 2016-block only varied economic_split and pool_committed_split
while fixing pool_ideology_strength and pool_max_loss_pct. This creates sampling bias that
prevents valid comparison of feature importance between 144-block and 2016-block regimes.

This LHS sweep samples all 4 key parameters proportionally to:
1. Provide unbiased feature importance estimates at 2016-block
2. Enable valid regime comparison with 144-block data
3. Detect interaction effects missed by targeted sweeps
4. Generate proper PRIM uncertainty bounds

DESIGN:
- 64 scenarios via Latin Hypercube Sampling (divisible by 4 for parallel namespaces)
- All 4 key parameters varied across their plausible ranges
- Fixed parameters set to validated values from Phase 1/2
- Duration 13000s at 2016-block retarget for realistic dynamics

RUNTIME: 64 scenarios x ~35 min = ~37 hours sequential
         With 4 namespaces: ~9.5 hours

RUN COMMAND:
  python tools/sweep/3_run_sweep.py \
    --input lhs_2016_full_parameter/build_manifest.json \
    --results-dir lhs_2016_full_parameter/results \
    --duration 13000 --retarget-interval 2016 --interval 2 \
    --namespace ns1 \
    --scenarios <scenario_list>
